name: FileCreate
applicable_version: Sysmon 1.0 and later; FileCreate logging must be enabled
definition: Sysmon Event ID 11 records file creation or overwrite activity and supports detection of payload staging, dropped malware, and suspicious persistence artifacts.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon generates the event when a configured process creates or overwrites a file. Collection rules determine which paths and file types are retained.

key_fields.explanation: The process that wrote the file. Join ProcessGuid to Event ID 1 for command line and parent context.
The created file path. Startup locations, temporary directories, user profiles, and script paths are common hunting areas.
The file creation timestamp recorded by Sysmon. Use it for timeline correlation rather than as proof of original file provenance.

false_positives: Browsers, installers, updaters, compilers, and endpoint agents create many files.
Temporary directories are noisy without process and extension filters.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

detection_notes: T1105 Ingress Tool Transfer triage is stronger when TargetFilename is an executable or script under C:\\Users\\ or C:\\ProgramData\\ and the same ProcessGuid has preceding network activity. T1547.001 persistence applies when the created file is under a per-user or all-users Startup folder; join ProcessGuid to Event ID 1 and confirm later execution of the same path.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 11
| where TargetFilename has_any ("\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup", "\\Users\\Public")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, TargetFilename, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet:
index=sysmon EventCode=11
| search TargetFilename IN ("*\\Startup\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*")
| table _time, host, Image, TargetFilename, ProcessGuid

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 11
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 11
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event and retains RenderedDescription.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 11
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=11
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before adding event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 11
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Event ID 11 is not globally unique; confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon before applying Sysmon field semantics.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 11
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 11
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 11
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: This is the per-user Startup folder path pattern.
value_references.security_relevance: A newly written executable or script here can run at user logon; verify the writer process and approved software installation context.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 11 record?
faqs.answer: Sysmon Event ID 11 records file creation or overwrite operations that match the active Sysmon FileCreate configuration and identifies the writing process and target path.

faqs.question: Does Sysmon Event ID 11 record every file creation?
faqs.answer: Only events selected by the installed Sysmon configuration are emitted; coverage and event volume therefore depend on include and exclude rules.

faqs.question: How can Event ID 11 support malware-staging detection?
faqs.answer: Correlate the writing ProcessGuid with network and process events, then prioritize executable or script files in user-writable, temporary, Startup, and other execution-relevant paths.

sources.title: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder (T1547.001)
